name: Ngrok Execution
description: Detects the execution of the Ngrok utility, which is used for port forwarding and protocol tunneling. Threat actors often use Ngrok to expose internal services to the internet, such as making RDP publicly accessible. This rule looks for specific command-line patterns indicative of malicious use.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that was executed, which should be 'ngrok.exe'.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute ngrok, including suspicious arguments.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which ngrok.exe was executed.

parameters.name: parent_process_name
parameters.description: The name of the parent process that launched ngrok.exe.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
